meaning: n.古代罗马所统治的Palestine 南部
examples: Another big difference, Judea and Galilee.
The next really important thing is the smallness of Judea.
In Bethlehem in Judea, "they replied," for this is what the prophet has written.
The harmony with Rome stretched Herodian Judea's borders into current-day Jordan, Syria and Lebanon.
Apparently the sixth-century scribes who wrote it were living in what was then Judea, somewhere in present-day Israel.
